skill.name: sales-automator
skill.description: Draft cold emails, follow-ups, and proposal templates. Creates pricing pages, case studies, and sales scripts. Use PROACTIVELY for sales outreach or lead nurturing.
skill.summary: Draft cold emails, follow-ups, and proposal templates. Creates pricing pages, case studies, and sale...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged a possible Ruby or shell backtick at SKILL.md:25 and a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md:3. Both are false positives: line 25 is a markdown code span around a documentation path, and line 3 is sales-oriented description text with no cryptographic operation. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, network access, executable script, or malicious intent was found in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Span Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: SKILL.md:25 uses backticks to format the path resources/implementation-playbook.md in markdown. It does not execute a shell command and provides no command injection vector.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is a markdown inline code span around a file path, not Ruby or shell syntax. No executable file, script block, or dynamic command construction exists in the reviewed skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Description Text Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: SKILL.md:3 is frontmatter description text about drafting cold emails and sales templates. It contains no cryptographic algorithm, hash call, encryption routine, or security-sensitive code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plain metadata describing sales writing tasks. There is no code or semantic evidence of weak cryptography.

content.value_statement: Sales teams lose time creating consistent outreach for every prospect and follow-up. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code draft emails, proposals, scripts, and testing ideas from clear sales inputs.
actual_capabilities: Drafts cold email sequences with three to five touchpoints.
Creates subject line options for A/B testing.
Suggests personalization variables for prospect research.
Builds follow-up schedules for lead nurturing.
Drafts proposal, quote, and pricing page copy.
Creates objection handling scripts for sales calls.
limitations: Does not send emails or connect to CRM systems by itself.
Requires accurate prospect, offer, and audience details from the user.
Does not verify legal compliance for outbound sales messages.
Does not guarantee response rates, conversions, or revenue outcomes.

use_cases.title: Build a Cold Outreach Sequence
use_cases.description: Create a concise multi-step email sequence with subject line tests, personalization fields, and a single call to action.

use_cases.title: Prepare Proposal Messaging
use_cases.description: Turn offer details into proposal sections, quote language, pricing page copy, and customer-focused benefits.

use_cases.title: Improve Lead Nurturing
use_cases.description: Plan follow-up timing, objection responses, and social proof angles for prospects who are not ready to buy.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic Cold Email
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a single prospect segment.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the sales-automator skill to draft a short cold email for [target audience]. The offer is [offer]. The main customer problem is [problem]. Include two subject lines and one clear call to action.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Follow-Up Sequence
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the sales-automator skill to create a four-touch follow-up sequence for prospects who opened the first email but did not reply. Include timing, email goals, subject line options, and personalization variables.

prompt_templates.title: Build Proposal and Objection Copy
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for active sales opportunities.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the sales-automator skill to draft proposal copy for [product or service]. Include pricing language, proof points, likely objections, and concise responses for a sales call.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Outreach Test Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for improving conversion data.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the sales-automator skill to design an A/B test plan for a cold email campaign. Compare subject lines, opening angles, calls to action, metrics, and decision rules.

output_examples.input: A founder needs outreach for a new analytics product aimed at ecommerce teams.
output: A three-email cold sequence focused on revenue leakage and faster reporting.
Six subject line options grouped by pain point, outcome, and curiosity.
Personalization fields for store platform, recent campaign, and current reporting workflow.

output_examples.input: An account executive needs help after a prospect raised pricing concerns.
output: A short response script that validates the concern and reframes value.
A follow-up email with proof points and a lower-friction next step.
Metrics to track, including reply rate, meeting conversion, and objection frequency.

best_practices: Provide the target audience, offer, customer pain, and desired next step before asking for copy.
Review every message for accuracy, compliance, and brand voice before using it with prospects.
Track reply quality, not only open rates, when judging outreach performance.
anti_patterns: Do not use generic outreach without prospect research or personalization.
Do not ask for misleading claims, fake urgency, or unsupported proof points.
Do not treat generated copy as legal compliance advice for outbound messaging.

faq.question: Can this skill send sales emails automatically?
faq.answer: No. It drafts outreach assets and plans. Sending requires a separate email or CRM tool.

faq.question: What inputs improve the results?
faq.answer: Provide the audience, offer, customer problem, proof points, tone, and desired call to action.

faq.question: Can it create full email sequences?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is designed to draft three to five touchpoint sequences and follow-up schedules.

faq.question: Does it help with sales calls?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft scripts, objection responses, and talking points for sales conversations.

faq.question: Can it guarantee more replies?
faq.answer: No. It can improve structure and testing quality, but results depend on list quality, offer, timing, and market fit.

faq.question: Should users review outputs before sending?
faq.answer: Yes. Users should check facts, claims, tone, and legal requirements before using any sales message.
